'''Tantajoc V''' was a [[Jungle/Legends|jungle]]-covered [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Tantajo system/Legends|Tantajo system]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] [[Halori sector/Legends|Halori sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> Tantajoc V [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] a binary [[Star/Legends|star]] and was completely uninhabited by [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]] as of [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], although its harsh and humid forests<ref name="wotc" /> teemed with dangerous predators.<ref name="marvel49" /> After fleeing a [[Great Revolt|coup]] on nearby [[Velmor]] in [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]], Velmorian crown [[Prince/Legends|prince]] [[Denid]] and his retainer [[Jedidiah]] crash-landed on Tantajoc V,<ref name="wotc">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20030306velmor|text=Velmor: Royalty and Rebellion|int=Velmor: Royalty and Rebellion}}</ref> where they survived for the next 20 years in a hostile environment. [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] leaders [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] tracked Denid's distress signal to Tantajoc V in 3 ABY, finally rescuing him from his [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]] and returning him to Velmor to assume his throne.<ref name="marvel49" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tantajoc V first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 49|''Star Wars'' (1977) 49]], written by [[Mike W. Barr]], illustrated by [[Walter Simonson]] and released in [[1981]].<ref name="marvel49" /> Although the world went unnamed in its original comic appearance, its name was given in [[2003]], as part of the [[Wizards of the Coast]] article ''[[Velmor: Royalty and Rebellion]]''.<ref name="wotc" />
